title: Difference between odd and even

content.problemStatement:
Write a program to accept as input the length and all the elements of an array

You need to output the difference between the sum of all the even index elements and the sum of the odd index elements.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N, the number of elements in the array. The next line contains N space‑separated integers representing the array (0‑based indexing).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single integer – the difference between the sum of elements at even indices and the sum of elements at odd indices.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Only index 0 (even) exists: 100. No odd indices. Difference=100-0=100.

solution.approach: We traverse each array once, adding the value to evenSum when the index is even and to oddSum when the index is odd. After processing the whole array the required answer is evenSum‑oddSum. This single pass gives the optimal O(N) time per test case and uses O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) for the whole input

tcGeneratorPython:
def generate_case(i, RINT, POWER):
    # i: test case index (0‑based) up to 20
    # RINT(a,b): random integer in [a,b]
    # POWER(a,b): a**b
    if i == 0:
        t=1
        print(t)
        n=1
        print(n)
        print(1)
    elif i == 1:
        t=1
        print(t)
        n=1
        print(n)
        print(1000)
    # Add further generation logic for other indices as needed.
